reasoning: The AAA corporate spread recently spiked to 0.59 in early April 2025 but has since shown strong downward momentum, falling linearly roughly 0.01 per day down to 0.42 on April 25. Given this sharp mean-reverison pattern, which mirrors a previous spike and rapid correction in August 2024 (0.47 down to 0.37), the value on the forecast date (April 27) will likely be around 0.41-0.42. Over the relatively short horizons of May 4 and May 27, the downward momentum is highly likely to continue correcting toward the low-to-mid 0.30s (which is the historical range for late 2024 and early 2025), making an increase above the April 27 baseline very unlikely. Over longer horizons (July 26 and October 24), there is more time for a new unexpected shock to cause another spread widening, meaning the probability should pull closer to 0.50, but since ~0.35 is mostly the baseline and 0.42 is already somewhat elevated (approximately the 80th-90th percentile over the past year), an increase remains less likely than a decrease.
